text: Design a local project picker/discovery flow for nearby .bowman repositories
description: Define and implement how the local service discovers or lets the user select repositories/workspaces with `.bowman`. The flow should fit local use, avoid hidden magic, and make switching projects explicit.

reflections.context: Make local project selection feel intentional by exposing nearby .bowman repositories instead of relying only on manual path entry and history.
reflections.outcome: The backend now provides discoverLocalProjects, and the selection view shows explicit nearby project suggestions plus the roots that were scanned.
reflections.effectiveness: A small backend scan plus a narrow selection-screen UI change delivered a concrete discovery flow without changing project loading semantics.
reflections.lesson: For local-first tools, explicit discover-and-choose flows are safer and easier to trust than silent auto-discovery.
reflections.pattern: This follows the same pattern as the header path work: orientation improves fastest when the UI makes local file-system context explicit.

status.comment: Added explicit local project discovery: backend now scans nearby roots for .bowman repositories and the project selection screen shows refreshable click-to-open suggestions with scanned roots visible.
